2605.18661.title: AI for Auto-Research: Roadmap & User Guide
2605.18661.paper_summary: AI-assisted research is crossing a threshold: fully automated systems can now generate research papers for as little as $15, while long-horizon agents can execute experiments, draft manuscripts, and simulate critique with minimal human input. Yet this productivity frontier exposes a deeper integrity problem: under scientific pressure, even frontier LLMs still fabricate results, miss hidden errors, and fail to judge novelty reliably. Studying developments through April 2026, we present an end-to-end analysis of AI across the complete research lifecycle, organized into four epistemological phases: Creation (idea generation, literature review, coding & experiments, tables & figures), Writing (paper writing), Validation (peer review, rebuttal & revision), and Dissemination (posters, slides, videos, social media, project pages, and interactive agents). We identify a sharp, stage-dependent boundary between reliable assistance and unreliable autonomy: AI excels at structured, retrieval-grounded, and tool-mediated tasks, but remains fragile for genuinely novel ideas, research-level experiments, and scientific judgment. Generated ideas often degrade after implementation, research code lags far behind pattern-matching benchmarks, and end-to-end autonomous systems have not yet consistently reached major-venue acceptance standards. We further show that greater automation can obscure rather than eliminate failure modes, making human-governed collaboration the most credible deployment paradigm. Finally, we provide a structured taxonomy, benchmark suite, and tool inventory, cross-stage design principles, and a practitioner-oriented playbook, with resources maintained at our project page.
2605.18661.paper_summary_zh: AI輔助研究正在跨越一個門檻：完全自動化的系統現在可以以低至15美元的價格生成研究論文，而長期代理可以執行實驗、起草手稿並模擬評審，幾乎不需要人類的輸入。<lb/>然而，這一生產力邊界暴露出更深層的誠信問題：在科學壓力下，即使是前沿的LLM也仍然會捏造結果、漏掉隱藏的錯誤，並且無法可靠地判斷新穎性。<lb/>通過研究到2026年4月的發展，我們提供了對AI在整個研究生命週期中的端到端分析，分為四個認識論階段：創造（想法生成、文獻回顧、編碼與實驗、表格與圖形）、寫作（論文寫作）、驗證（同行評審、反駁與修訂）和傳播（海報、幻燈片、視頻、社交媒體、項目頁面和互動代理）。<lb/>我們確定了一個明確的、依賴於階段的邊界，區分可靠的輔助和不可靠的自主性：AI在結構化、基於檢索和工具介導的任務中表現出色，但在真正的新穎想法、研究級實驗和科學判斷方面仍然脆弱。<lb/>生成的想法在實施後往往會退化，研究代碼遠遠落後於模式匹配基準，且端到端的自動系統尚未穩定地達到主要會議的接受標準。<lb/>我們進一步顯示，更多的自動化可能會掩蓋而不是消除失敗模式，使得以人類為主導的協作成為最可信的部署範式。<lb/>最後，我們提供了一個結構化的分類法、基準套件和工具清單、跨階段設計原則以及面向實踐者的行動手冊，資源維護在我們的項目頁面上。
2605.18661.author: Lingdong Kong et.al.
2605.18661.authors: Lingdong Kong, Xian Sun, Wei Chow, Linfeng Li, Kevin Qinghong Lin, Xuan Billy Zhang, Song Wang, Rong Li, Qing Wu, Wei Gao, Yingshuo Wang, Shaoyuan Xie, Jiachen Liu, Leigang Qu, Shijie Li, Lai Xing Ng, Benoit R. Cottereau, Ziwei Liu, Tat-Seng Chua, Wei Tsang Ooi
